The concept of single node interventions is related to interventional distributions Z (i) as mentioned in Assumption 3. In this context, single node interventions are considered for each intervention i in the latent distribution Z.

The concept of single node interventions is directly related to Assumption 3 in the context. Assumption 3 discusses the consideration of interventional distributions Z (i) which are single node interventions of the latent distribution Z.
The Intervention Types are defined in relation to perfect (or stochastic hard) interventions. A perfect intervention is a type of intervention mentioned in the context.
A perfect intervention is defined as removing all connections to former parents and potentially changing variance and mean of the noise variable.
A pure noise intervention targets a node with parents by changing only the noise distribution through a change of variance or a mean shift.
A single-node intervention is imperfect if it is not perfect and may be referred to as a soft intervention in some works.
An imperfect intervention is defined as not being perfect and necessarily changing the relation to the parents.
An exhaustive set of interventions is required according to the context.
Assumption is made that all nodes are intervened upon by at least one intervention to ensure coverage.
